items.summary: A cross-sectional survey of physical therapists treating pediatric and adolescent ACL injury patients aimed to describe factors influencing clinical decision-making for blood flow restriction training (BFRT) in this population.
items.curatedReason: 本调查提示，儿科和青少年 ACL 损伤患者的 BFRT 临床决策受多种因素影响。如果你在考虑使用 BFRT，需留意目前该领域临床实践的现状、现有挑战以及研究与实践间的可能差距。
items.titleZh: 影响前交叉韧带损伤后儿童和青少年患者血流限制训练临床决策的因素
items.summaryZh: 一项针对治疗儿童和青少年 ACL 损伤患者的物理治疗师的横断面调查，旨在描述影响该人群血流限制训练（BFRT）临床决策的因素。
items.curatedReasonEn: This survey suggests that clinical decision-making for BFRT in pediatric and adolescent ACL injury patients is influenced by multiple factors. If you are considering BFRT, be mindful of the current state of clinical practice, existing challenges, and potential gaps between research and practice in this population.
items.limitation: 本研究为横断面调查，依赖于物理治疗师的自我报告，可能存在回忆偏倚和报告偏倚。
items.limitationEn: This cross-sectional survey relies on physical therapists' self-reports, which may be subject to recall and reporting bias.
items.journal: International journal of sports physical therapy

items.title: The Perspective and Needs of Older Adults With Osteoporosis With a Recent Upper Extremity Fragility Fracture Concerning Fall Risk and Fear of Falling.
items.summary: A qualitative study explored the perspectives of older adults with osteoporosis and upper extremity fragility fractures, highlighting their health concerns, the impact of falls and fear of falling (FoF), and views on rehabilitation, emphasizing FoF as a psychological challenge often overlooked.
items.curatedReason: 老年骨质疏松患者上肢脆性骨折后，跌倒风险和跌倒恐惧是普遍存在的心理和社会挑战，且常被忽视。这项定性研究提醒你，在制定这些患者的康复计划时，必须深入了解其跌倒恐惧，并将其纳入干预策略，以提升康复依从性和效果。
items.titleZh: 上肢脆性骨折老年骨质疏松患者对跌倒风险和跌倒恐惧的观点和需求
items.summaryZh: 一项定性研究探讨了上肢脆性骨折老年骨质疏松患者的观点，突出了他们对健康、跌倒及跌倒恐惧（FoF）的影响，以及对康复的看法，强调 FoF 是一个常被忽视的心理挑战。
items.curatedReasonEn: Fall risk and fear of falling are prevalent and often overlooked psychological and social challenges in older adults with osteoporosis after upper extremity fragility fractures. This qualitative study reminds you that when developing rehabilitation plans for these patients, it is crucial to thoroughly understand and incorporate their fear of falling into intervention strategies to improve rehabilitation adherence and outcomes.
items.limitation: 本研究为定性研究，其发现的观点和需求可能无法代表所有老年骨质疏松患者。
items.limitationEn: This qualitative study's findings regarding perspectives and needs may not represent all older adults with osteoporosis.

items.title: Effects of Dynamic Taping on Dynamic Balance and Proprioception in Recreational Athletes with Chronic Ankle Instability.
items.summary: A single-blind RCT (n=28) found dynamic taping and sports taping had similar immediate effects on dynamic balance and proprioception in recreational athletes with chronic ankle instability, suggesting dynamic taping as a viable alternative.
items.curatedReason: 动态贴扎（DT）和运动贴扎（ST）在改善慢性踝关节不稳（CAI）患者的动态平衡和本体感觉方面具有相似的即时效果。这表明 DT 是 ST 的有效替代方案，特别是在需要保持功能性活动而又不限制运动的场景。你可以根据患者需求和贴扎目标选择合适的贴扎技术。
items.titleZh: 动态贴扎对慢性踝关节不稳休闲运动员动态平衡和本体感觉的影响
items.summaryZh: 一项单盲随机对照试验（n=28）发现，动态贴扎和运动贴扎对慢性踝关节不稳的休闲运动员在动态平衡和本体感觉方面具有相似的即时效果，表明动态贴扎是一个可行的替代方案。
items.curatedReasonEn: Dynamic Taping (DT) and Sports Taping (ST) show similar immediate effects in improving dynamic balance and proprioception in patients with chronic ankle instability (CAI). This suggests DT is an effective alternative to ST, especially when maintaining functional motion without restricting movement is desired. You can choose the appropriate taping technique based on patient needs and taping goals.
items.limitation: 研究样本量较小（28 名参与者），且仅评估了短期即时效应。
items.limitationEn: The study involved a small sample size (28 participants) and only assessed immediate short-term effects.
items.journal: International journal of sports physical therapy

items.title: Biomechanical effects of Kinesio versus Mulligan ankle taping on balance and ankle function in chronic STROKE: A randomized controlled trial.
items.summary: A randomized controlled trial (n=33) found Kinesio taping or Mulligan taping combined with treadmill training significantly improved ankle dorsiflexion range of motion, balance, and fall efficacy in chronic stroke patients over six weeks, compared to treadmill training alone.
items.curatedReason: 在慢性卒中患者中，Kinesio 贴扎或 Mulligan 贴扎结合跑台训练，在踝关节背屈 ROM、平衡和跌倒效能方面优于单独的跑台训练。这提示你，将贴扎技术整合到卒中患者的康复方案中，可能有助于提升其步态和平衡功能，降低跌倒风险。
items.titleZh: Kinesio 贴扎与 Mulligan 踝关节贴扎对慢性卒中患者平衡和踝关节功能的生物力学效应：一项随机对照试验
items.summaryZh: 一项随机对照试验（n=33）发现，Kinesio 贴扎或 Mulligan 贴扎结合跑台训练，在六周内显著改善了慢性卒中患者的踝关节背屈活动度、平衡和跌倒效能，优于单独的跑台训练。
items.curatedReasonEn: In chronic stroke patients, Kinesio taping or Mulligan taping combined with treadmill training improved ankle dorsiflexion ROM, balance, and fall efficacy more than treadmill training alone. This suggests that integrating taping techniques into stroke rehabilitation protocols may help enhance gait and balance function, reducing fall risk.
items.limitation: 研究样本量较小（每组 11 人），且干预周期为 6 周，尚不能评估长期效果。
items.limitationEn: The study had a small sample size (11 participants per group) and a 6-week intervention period, making it unable to assess long-term effects.

items.title: Validity and reliability of on‑site and off‑site direct observation of procedural skills (DOPS) for knee mobilization in Physical therapy education.
items.summary: A study validated the Direct Observation of Procedural Skills (DOPSknee) tool for knee joint mobilization, showing good inter-rater reliability for both on-site and off-site assessments among 42 physical therapy students.
items.curatedReason: 这份研究验证了 DOPSknee 工具在膝关节松动术教学中的有效性与可靠性，无论是在现场还是远程评估，都能提供即时反馈。如果你负责 PT 教育或临床带教，可以考虑将此工具整合到你的培训和评估体系中。
items.titleZh: 现场与远程直接观察操作技能（DOPS）在物理治疗教育中膝关节松动术的效度与信度
items.summaryZh: 一项研究验证了膝关节松动术操作技能直接观察工具（DOPSknee）的效度与信度，显示该工具在 42 名物理治疗学生中，无论现场还是远程评估，均具有良好的评估者间信度。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study validates the DOPSknee tool for teaching knee joint mobilization, demonstrating its reliability and validity for immediate feedback in both on-site and off-site assessments. If you are involved in PT education or clinical supervision, consider integrating this tool into your training and evaluation system.
items.limitation: 研究样本量较小（42 名学生），可能影响结果的普遍性。
items.limitationEn: The study involved a relatively small sample size (42 students), which may affect the generalizability of the findings.
items.journal: The Journal of manual & manipulative therapy

items.title: TRAK MSK: a randomised external feasibility trial and process evaluation of a digital physiotherapy supported self-management programme for people with musculoskeletal pain.
items.summary: A two-arm multi-site randomized external feasibility trial of TRAK MSK, a digital physiotherapy-supported self-management program for musculoskeletal pain, explored the feasibility of conducting a definitive trial, randomizing participants to the program or usual NHS physiotherapy care over 16 weeks.
items.curatedReason: TRAK MSK 项目显示了数字物理治疗支持的自我管理对慢性肌肉骨骼疼痛患者的潜力。这项可行性研究为未来大规模 RCT 铺平了道路，预示着数字健康解决方案在改善患者功能和生活质量方面的作用日益增强。你可以留意后续的决定性试验结果。
items.titleZh: TRAK MSK：一项针对肌肉骨骼疼痛患者的数字物理治疗支持自我管理计划的随机外部可行性试验与过程评估
items.summaryZh: 一项针对肌肉骨骼疼痛患者的数字物理治疗支持自我管理计划（TRAK MSK）的两臂多中心随机外部可行性试验，评估了开展决定性试验的可行性，参与者被随机分配到 TRAK MSK 干预组或常规 NHS 物理治疗护理组，为期 16 周。
items.curatedReasonEn: The TRAK MSK program demonstrates the potential of digitally supported self-management for individuals with chronic musculoskeletal pain. This feasibility study paves the way for future large-scale RCTs, indicating a growing role for digital health solutions in improving patient function and quality of life. Keep an eye out for the results of the definitive trial.
items.limitation: 这是一项可行性研究，其结果不能直接推断为干预措施的最终疗效。
items.limitationEn: This is a feasibility study, and its results cannot be directly extrapolated to the definitive efficacy of the intervention.

items.title: Major discrepancies in recommendations regarding long-term activity restrictions following knee replacement: a survey among Swedish physiotherapists.
items.summary: A survey among Swedish physiotherapists revealed major discrepancies in recommendations for long-term activity restrictions following knee replacement surgery due to limited evidence, potentially impacting patient confidence and rehabilitation adherence.
items.curatedReason: 膝关节置换术后长期活动限制建议存在巨大差异，这表明该领域缺乏统一的循证指导。你在为患者提供指导时，需要认识到这种不确定性，并根据个体情况进行权衡，等待更明确的证据出炉。
items.titleZh: 膝关节置换术后长期活动限制建议存在重大分歧：瑞典物理治疗师调查
items.summaryZh: 一项针对瑞典物理治疗师的调查显示，由于缺乏证据，膝关节置换术后长期活动限制的建议存在重大差异，这可能影响患者信心和康复依从性。
items.curatedReasonEn: Significant discrepancies exist in recommendations for long-term activity restrictions after knee replacement, indicating a lack of uniform evidence-based guidance in this area. When advising patients, you should acknowledge this uncertainty and weigh recommendations based on individual circumstances, awaiting clearer evidence.
items.limitation: 本研究为问卷调查，可能存在回忆偏倚和报告偏倚，且仅限于瑞典物理治疗师的观点。
items.limitationEn: This survey study may be subject to recall and reporting bias, and is limited to the perspectives of Swedish physiotherapists.

items.title: Adapting the MSK-HQ for paediatric musculoskeletal care: Insights from a national survey and focus group of physiotherapists.
items.summary: A national survey and focus group with 55 UK pediatric clinicians (mostly physiotherapists) revealed positive views on adapting the Musculoskeletal Health Questionnaire (MSK-HQ) for use in pediatric musculoskeletal care for patients aged 8-18 years.
items.curatedReason: MSK-HQ 在儿科肌肉骨骼护理中的适应性得到了英国临床医生的认可，显示出其在该群体中作为有效结局测量工具的潜力。如果你在儿科 MSK 领域执业，可以关注其进一步的验证和标准化，以便未来将其整合到你的临床评估中。
items.titleZh: 针对儿科肌肉骨骼护理调整 MSK-HQ：来自全国物理治疗师调查和焦点小组的见解
items.summaryZh: 一项针对 55 名英国儿科临床医生（多数为物理治疗师）的全国调查和焦点小组显示，他们对将肌肉骨骼健康问卷（MSK-HQ）调整用于 8-18 岁儿科肌肉骨骼护理持积极看法。
items.curatedReasonEn: The adaptability of MSK-HQ in pediatric musculoskeletal care is recognized by UK clinicians, showing its potential as an effective outcome measure in this population. If you practice in pediatric MSK, you can monitor its further validation and standardization for potential integration into your clinical assessments.
items.limitation: 本研究为问卷调查和焦点小组形式，存在主观性和潜在的招募偏倚，不代表全国范围的意见。
items.limitationEn: This study, using surveys and focus groups, is subject to subjectivity and potential recruitment bias, and does not represent nationwide opinions.

items.title: Cognitive Rehabilitation Improves Functional Outcomes in Adults With Post-COVID Cognitive Impairment: Study
items.summary: A randomized clinical trial found that cognitive rehabilitation led to significantly greater and sustained improvements in functional goal attainment compared to usual care in adults with post-COVID cognitive impairment.
items.curatedReason: 这项 RCT 确认认知康复能显著改善 COVID-19 后认知障碍患者的功能结局，并且效果持续。您可以将其作为这类患者的标准干预措施，并积极推荐。
items.titleZh: 研究发现认知康复可改善 COVID-19 后认知障碍成年患者的功能结局
items.summaryZh: 一项随机临床试验发现，与常规护理相比，认知康复能显著且持续改善 COVID-19 后认知障碍成年患者的功能目标达成。
items.curatedReasonEn: This RCT confirms that cognitive rehabilitation significantly improves functional outcomes in patients with post-COVID cognitive impairment, with sustained effects. You can integrate this as a standard intervention and actively recommend it for these patients.
items.limitation: 研究可能存在特定人群的纳入排除标准，外推时需谨慎。
items.limitationEn: The study may have specific inclusion/exclusion criteria for its population; caution is needed when extrapolating.

items.title: Use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissue: guidelines from the American Society of Regional Anesthesia and Pain Medicine, the American Academy of Pain Medicine, the American Society of Interventional Pain Physicians, and the International Pain and Spine Intervention Society
items.summary: This provides guidelines from multiple American societies on the use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissue.
items.curatedReason: 这些来自多个主要学会的注射疗法指南为您提供了最新的循证实践框架，帮助您评估皮质类固醇注射在肌肉骨骼软组织和关节中的应用。注意其中的禁忌症和安全考量。
items.titleZh: 美国区域麻醉和疼痛医学学会、美国疼痛医学学会等关于关节和肌肉骨骼软组织皮质类固醇注射使用和安全性的指南
items.summaryZh: 这份指南来自多个美国学会，提供了关于关节和肌肉骨骼软组织中皮质类固醇注射的使用和安全性建议。
items.curatedReasonEn: These guidelines from major societies offer an up-to-date, evidence-based framework for corticosteroid injections in musculoskeletal soft tissue and joints. Pay attention to contraindications and safety considerations.
